verdict.answer_first: B states the actionable default immediately: “Assume "seconds-stale is fine" → TTL-based cache-aside with Redis”; A opens with “decision path” and puts concrete default later.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits to one default: “TTL-based cache-aside with Redis”; A surveys options like “In-process,” “Shared cache,” “TTL only,” and “Write-through / explicit bust.”
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on now because it gives TTLs, Redis, jitter, lock, and flag rollout in one first bullet; A is solid but requires walking through more decision steps first.
